executiveSummary.text: Regime is risk-on but rotating: US futures reopened firmer after the US holiday (ES +0.5%, NQ +1.4%), while the last US cash session showed equal-weight S&P +0.7% beating cap-weight SPY -0.1% and QQQ -1.7%.
Rates are not giving a full all-clear: latest Treasury curve before the holiday was 2Y 4.14%, 10Y 4.49%, 30Y 4.98%; DXY is steady near 100.9. Liquidity/rate shock risk remains medium, not low.

AI/semis had an abnormal de-risking day: SOXX -5.6%, SMH -4.5%; KL holdings LRCX -10.2%, KLAC -11.5%, AMAT -7.4%, ASML -4.0%, MU -5.5%, SNPS -3.8%. Fresh searches attribute this mainly to sector-wide profit-taking/unwind rather than company-specific thesis break.

Scarcity assets are firm: gold futures +2.2%, silver +4.9%, copper +2.0%; GLD +2.0%, SLV +2.7%, COPX +1.8%. The useful read-through is real-asset scarcity plus potential real-yield/dollar sensitivity, not a simplistic crisis bid.

Singapore remains constructive: STI +0.5% to 5,244, banks modestly positive, REITs stable-to-higher. Sembcorp -3.4% was the main local abnormal holding move and should be watched but not overinterpreted absent fresh thesis-damaging news.

Base case: constructive but choppier bull market 55%; valuation/rates-led correction 30%; liquidity/geopolitical shock 15%. Confidence: medium because US cash data is holiday-stale while futures/commodities are fresh.

usGlobal.what: Risk appetite remains intact, but leadership rotated away from mega-cap/semis in the last cash session.
usGlobal.why: QQQ fell 1.7% and SOXX fell 5.6%, while equal-weight S&P rose 0.7% and Dow rose 1.1%; futures reopened firmer after the holiday.
usGlobal.implication: Bullish breadth broadening is healthy if credit stays calm, but AI-heavy portfolios can underperform even in a rising market.

ratesMacro.what: Curve remains high and mildly steep at the long end.
ratesMacro.why: 2Y 4.14%, 10Y 4.49%, 30Y 4.98% before the holiday; DXY stable near 100.9.
ratesMacro.implication: Duration-sensitive REITs and long-duration tech still need a benign inflation/Fed path; cash at ~4% remains useful ballast.

asiaSingapore.what: Singapore equities are still supported by banks and yield stability.
asiaSingapore.why: STI +0.5%; DBS +0.5%, OCBC +0.9%, UOB +0.4%; major REITs mostly flat-to-positive.
asiaSingapore.implication: SG dividend sleeve remains portfolio ballast; don’t chase banks after strength unless yields and credit costs stay supportive.

commodities.why: Gold +2.2%, silver +4.9%, copper +2.0%; WTI/Brent soft near $68/$72 as fresh headlines discuss glut risk after easing supply fears.
commodities.implication: Scarcity thesis is working through metals; oil is less useful as a broad inflation hedge if supply overwhelms demand.

geopolitics.what: Oil/geopolitical transmission currently points more to supply/glut relief than shock premium.
geopolitics.why: Fresh Bloomberg/Yahoo headline cites oil reversal and glut fears after US-Iran peace/supply normalization; shipping-risk headlines are not yet showing a crude spike.
geopolitics.implication: Lower oil can ease inflation expectations and long yields, indirectly helping duration assets; energy equities may lag if crude stays capped.

moves.what: Semicap/equipment sleeve abnormal selloff: LRCX -10.2%, KLAC -11.5%, AMAT -7.4%, ASML -4.0%; SOXX -5.6%.
moves.why: Fresh search results describe a broad semiconductor unwind/profit-taking after a major rally; no single company-specific impairment was identified in last-24h scan.
moves.implication: This hits KL’s AI/Semicaps sleeve but does not by itself damage the scarcity thesis; the real invalidator would be AI capex cuts, foundry order cancellations, or margin/order-book deterioration.
moves.action: HOLD / staged ADD only if the selloff extends another 8-12% without earnings-guide deterioration.

moves.what: Memory/AI compute volatility: MU -5.5%; related fresh headline says AI has made memory one of the tightest markets, while SK Hynix seeks a large US listing.
moves.why: Profit-taking hit high-beta AI memory after a strong run; HBM/memory tightness remains a second-order beneficiary of AI capex.
moves.implication: Supports KL’s memory/HBM scarcity thesis, but position sizing must respect cyclicality.
moves.action: HOLD; ADD only on guidance-confirmed HBM demand, not just price weakness.

moves.why: Likely caught in semiconductor/long-duration software de-risking; no fresh thesis-breaking EDA news found.
moves.implication: EDA remains a toll-road exposure to chip complexity; weakness is more valuation/positioning unless design starts or regulatory issues worsen.
moves.action: HOLD / WATCH earnings commentary on AI design activity and China restrictions.

moves.what: META -4.9% while MSFT +1.6%; hyperscaler dispersion widened.
moves.why: Fresh search shows Meta AI reorganization/compute initiatives but no clear one-day thesis break; market may be rotating within megacap tech.
moves.implication: AI capex monetization discipline matters: hyperscalers that convert capex into cloud/ads cash flow will be rewarded; perceived AI spend without ROI will be punished.
moves.action: HOLD; WATCH next earnings for capex-to-revenue conversion.

moves.why: Fresh market data and search point to quality/financial toll-road bid; Visa also had fresh analyst support/52-week-high commentary.
moves.implication: Validates KL’s financial toll-road sleeve as non-AI compounder ballast.
moves.action: HOLD; TRIM only if position weight overshoots target after sharp rerating.

moves.what: Sembcorp -3.4%, main Singapore abnormal holding move.
moves.why: No fresh thesis-damaging headline found in last-24h scan; likely local positioning/noise unless company news emerges.
moves.implication: Renewables/power exposure remains strategically tied to electrification, but single-name volatility should not dominate the Singapore ballast sleeve.
moves.action: WATCH; invalidator is deterioration in project returns, leverage, or policy support.

moves.why: Metals futures rallied with silver/copper leading; likely a mix of real-asset scarcity, industrial demand and dollar/real-yield sensitivity.
moves.implication: Supports KL’s scarcity assets sleeve; silver/copper are more pro-cyclical than gold, so they confirm risk appetite more than pure fear.
moves.action: HOLD; ADD copper miners only on pullbacks or confirmed supply-demand squeeze, not vertical moves.

thesisImpact.what: AI scarcity thesis had a price shock, not a thesis shock.
thesisImpact.why: The selloff centered on crowded winners/equipment, while no fresh evidence showed AI capex cancellation.
thesisImpact.implication: Use the cash barbell to be a liquidity provider only if fundamentals remain intact.

thesisImpact.why: Cash yields ~4%, SG banks/REITs stable, metals up, while AI sleeve absorbed volatility.
thesisImpact.implication: No need to sell quality AI assets into a positioning unwind.
thesisImpact.action: IGNORE day-to-day noise unless earnings change.

narratives.title: AI winners are being sorted, not abandoned
narratives.description: Fresh CNBC/Yahoo-style headlines emphasize the hunt for next AI winners; the price action says market is rotating from crowded semicap winners into broader quality/cyclicals.
narratives.source: Brave Search last-24h results: CNBC/Yahoo/TIKR snippets
narratives.priceImpact: Near-term negative for crowded semis; constructive if earnings confirm capex durability.

narratives.title: Metals scarcity is diverging from oil
narratives.description: Gold/silver/copper rallied while oil stayed soft on glut-risk headlines. That is not classic generic inflation trade; it is a bifurcation between electrification/monetary metals and crude supply normalization.
narratives.priceImpact: Supports KL scarcity sleeve, but oil-energy equities may not participate.

narratives.title: Singapore as ballast, not rocket fuel
narratives.description: STI at 5,244 with banks steady and REITs firm gives KL income/balance-sheet resilience while US tech breathes out.
narratives.source: Yahoo Finance Singapore/Business Times snippets and price data
narratives.priceImpact: Lower volatility support; limited upside if rates rise again.

secondOrderEffects.text: If oil stays soft, headline inflation pressure can ease; 2nd order: lower inflation expectations reduce pressure on long yields; 3rd order: REITs and long-duration tech recover even without a Fed cut.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Watch whether crude weakness feeds breakevens/yields rather than assuming commodities all move together.

secondOrderEffects.text: Semicap selloff can tighten financial conditions for AI supply-chain equities; 2nd order: weaker equity currency can affect M&A/capex sentiment; 3rd order: if hyperscalers reaffirm capex, the selloff becomes a buying opportunity.
secondOrderEffects.implication: The next hyperscaler/equipment earnings calls matter more than today’s price move.

secondOrderEffects.text: Broadening from QQQ to RSP/Dow lowers index fragility; 2nd order: KL’s VWRA/SG dividend sleeves cushion AI volatility; 3rd order: if breadth broadening occurs with credit calm, it extends the cycle.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Portfolio construction is doing its job; avoid overreacting to one crowded sleeve.

secondOrderEffects.text: A stable DXY near 100.9 reduces immediate USD funding stress; 2nd order: EM/Asia risk can stay supported; 3rd order: a sudden USD/JPY spike would revive carry-unwind risk.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Keep yen/carry on risk watch despite calm headline VIX.

investment.thesis: Staged adds to semiconductor equipment/EDA only if fundamentals remain intact.
investment.trigger: SOXX/SMH or KL equipment names fall another 8-12% while ASML/LRCX/KLAC/AMAT order books and AI capex commentary remain stable.
investment.whyNow: Crowded AI winners just had a forced-looking unwind; KL has cash ballast and the long-run bottleneck thesis remains plausible.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, foundry utilization deterioration, export-control hit bigger than expected, or margin guide-down.

investment.thesis: Keep SG banks/quality REITs as income ballast, not momentum chase.
investment.trigger: Add REITs only if 10Y yield breaks lower toward/under ~4.25% or spreads widen without balance-sheet stress.
investment.whyNow: STI/banks are strong; discipline matters after local strength.
investment.invalidator: Credit-cost spike, recessionary rental pressure, or renewed long-yield surge.

trading.thesis: Mean-reversion bounce in semis is possible after abnormal drawdown.
trading.trigger: SMH/SOXX reclaim prior-day breakdown levels with yields and DXY stable.
trading.whyNow: Futures reopened firmer and selloff looks positioning-led.
trading.invalidator: Further broad market deterioration with HYG down and VIX >20.

trading.thesis: Metals momentum is strong but chase risk is high.
trading.trigger: Prefer pullbacks to rising 20-day trend or copper confirmation above recent highs with inventories tightening.
trading.whyNow: Gold/silver/copper all crossed material move thresholds.
trading.invalidator: Real yields and USD rise together, or China/industrial demand weakens.

watchlist.thesis: Fed/rates path is the main macro swing factor.
watchlist.trigger: 10Y >4.65-4.75% with DXY rising, or 10Y <4.25% with breakevens contained.
watchlist.whyNow: Long end remains near 5% on 30Y; valuation duration risk is not gone.

watchlist.trigger: MSFT/AMZN/GOOG/META earnings: capex growth must map to cloud/ads/revenue growth, not just depreciation burden.
watchlist.whyNow: META weakness vs MSFT strength shows market is discriminating.
watchlist.invalidator: All hyperscalers reaffirm ROI and margins expand.

riskWatch.name: AI valuation/crowding unwind
riskWatch.description: KL’s AI/Semicaps sleeve saw multiple >3% moves; a positioning unwind can persist even without thesis damage.
riskWatch.mitigation: Use staged adds, do not average down mechanically; require earnings/order-book confirmation.

riskWatch.name: Rates/real-yield shock
riskWatch.description: 10Y 4.49% and 30Y 4.98% leave duration-sensitive tech/REITs exposed if inflation or term premium rises.
riskWatch.mitigation: Maintain cash/CPF ballast; add duration-sensitive assets only when yields stabilize.

riskWatch.name: Oil/geopolitics regime flip
riskWatch.description: Current oil signal is glut/relief, but shipping or policy shocks could reverse crude and inflation expectations quickly.
riskWatch.mitigation: Watch Brent >$78-$80 plus DXY/yields reaction; avoid simplistic safe-haven assumptions.

riskWatch.name: Yen/carry/liquidity shock
riskWatch.description: USD/JPY around 161 keeps carry-trade vulnerability alive even with VIX calm.
riskWatch.mitigation: Monitor JPY strength spikes and credit ETFs; keep dry powder.

keyEvents.text: US cash market data is holiday-stale; futures reopened firmer on Jul 5/6 Asia time.
Fresh last-24h searches show broad semiconductor unwind/profit-taking headlines across LRCX/KLAC/AMAT and AI memory discussion.
keyEvents.source: Brave Search: TIKR/MEXC/24/7 Wall St/Fortune snippets
US Treasury Daily Yield Curve, Jul 2

keyEvents.text: Oil headlines emphasize glut risk after supply normalization; WTI near $68.
STI closed at 5,244.29; Singapore banks modestly positive, REITs broadly stable.

bottomLine.whatMatters: The real signal is not “AI is broken”; it is a crowded AI/semicap unwind inside a still-constructive, broadening risk regime, with rates still the key constraint.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold core VWRA/SG dividend/AI scarcity exposures; use cash only in staged tranches if semicap fundamentals remain intact; keep metals as scarcity hedge but avoid chasing vertical silver/copper moves.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: One-day semiconductor price shock without order-book/capex evidence; generic crisis/gold narratives; portfolio-only noise that distracts from rates, breadth, USD and earnings quality.
